sections.paragraph:
δογματίζω (s. δόγμα) 1 aor. ἐδογμάτισα LXX; pf. δεδογμάτικα 1 Esdr 6:33; pf. pass. ptc. δεδογματισμένον 3 Macc 4:11; 1 aor. pass. ἐδογματίσθη (=decree, ordain, since II b.c.; s. Nägeli 32; Da 2:13 and elsewhere LXX, Just., Tat.; Ath.: ‘state position’ or ‘viewpoint’) to put under obligation by rules or ordinances, obligate: pass. submit to rules and regulations Col 2:20 here permissive pass. (s. Gildersleeve, Syntax I 73)=permit yourselves to be put under etc. τὰ δεδογματισμένα ὑπό τινος things decreed by someone 1 Cl 20:4; 27:5 (cp. 3 Macc 4:11).—M-M. DELG s.v. δοκάω etc. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Gildersleeve, Gildersleeve, Syntax = BG./CMiller, Syntax of Classical Greek—List 6

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 254
